name: Length Constraint — Round Cells vs Rods
blurb: Length (elongation) constraint — CC3D's LengthConstraint, added to the CPM core. The SAME lawn of identical square cells is relaxed twice. LEFT: no constraint, so surface tension rounds each cell into a compact blob (cobblestone epithelium). RIGHT: a per-cell spring E = λ·(ℓ − ℓ_target)² pulls each cell's major-axis length (largest eigenvalue of its gyration tensor) toward a target well above the round value, so cells thin and elongate into randomly oriented rods — a lawn of rod bacteria — while volume is conserved. Length is tracked incrementally, so the constraint costs O(1) per flip.
